questions.question: Cholecystokinin (CCK) stimulates:

options.B: Contraction of gall bladder and release of bile

questions.question: The diagram shows different parts of the human digestive system. What is the pH at which pepsin works most effectively?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of human digestive system showing esophagus, stomach, small intestine (duodenum, jejunum, ileum), large intestine, with a pH scale alongside each segment: mouth (6.5–7.5), stomach (1.5–3.5), duodenum (6–7), jejunum and ileum (7–8), colon (5.5–7)

questions.question: Crypts of Lieberkühn are found in:
Which of the following is correctly matched?
The absorption of glucose and amino acids in the small intestine occurs by:
The diagram shows the process of fat digestion. What are the small droplets formed during emulsification called?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram showing fat digestion process: large fat globule (triglyceride), bile salts acting as emulsifying agents surrounding fat, formation of small micelles/emulsion droplets, pancreatic lipase acting on surface, release of fatty acids and monoglycerides, reassembly into chylomicrons in enterocyte, exocytosis into lacteal

questions.question: Which vitamin is synthesized by intestinal bacteria?
Gastrin hormone stimulates:

options.B: Secretion of gastric juice (HCl and pepsinogen)

questions.question: The diagram shows the structure of the stomach wall. Identify the cells that secrete HCl.
questions.diagram_description: Cross-section of gastric mucosa showing gastric pit opening to surface, mucous neck cells near top of glands, parietal (oxyntic) cells (large, pale, in middle region of gland secreting HCl), chief (zymogenic) cells (smaller, darker, at base secreting pepsinogen), enteroendocrine cells at base

questions.question: The enzyme sucrase splits sucrose into:
Achalasia is a disorder of:
The diagram shows absorption mechanisms across the intestinal epithelium. Which molecule uses co-transport with Na+ for absorption?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of intestinal epithelial cell showing apical membrane (brush border) facing intestinal lumen and basolateral membrane facing blood. Na+/Glucose co-transporter (SGLT1) on apical membrane, GLUT2 on basolateral membrane, Na+/K+ ATPase on basolateral membrane, amino acid transporters, fatty acid diffusion, fructose via GLUT5

questions.question: Kupffer cells are found in:
Which digestive juice has no enzyme?
The diagram shows the liver lobule. Which vessel receives oxygenated blood?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of liver lobule showing: central vein at center, hepatocytes arranged in radiating cords, sinusoids (liver capillaries) between cords, portal triad at corners consisting of hepatic artery (small), portal vein (large), and bile duct; Kupffer cells lining sinusoids; bile canaliculi between hepatocytes draining to bile duct

questions.question: Oesophagus? yes. it is a thin, long tube which extends posteriorly passing through thorax and diaphragm.
Gastro-oesophageal sphincter regulates the opening of oesophagus into the stomach.
Jejunum? yes. long coiled middle portion of small intestine.
Ileum? yes. highly coiled portion that opens into large intestine.
Caecum is a small blind sac which hosts some symbiotic micro-organisms.
